channel.title: forensics on Analyst
channel.description: Recent content in forensics on Analyst

item.title: GCFA
item.description: This is my GCFA study plan. I hope it will help me in organizing and prioritising the topics to learn and also in estimating the time I need to get ready.

item.title: GCFA Study Plan
item.description: This is my GCFA study plan. I hope it will help me in organizing and prioritising the topics to learn and also in estimating the time I need to get ready.

item.title: Investigation Layout
item.description: Anatomy of an investigation: Identification/Assessment. Understand your scope (technical and legal aspects), your goals and people you are going to work with.

item.title: First Response
item.description: Anatomy of an investigation: Identification/Assessment. Understand your scope (technical and legal aspects), your goals and people you are going to work with.

item.title: Network Triage
item.description: Intro Ports idetify services (usually, but not always). System ports are forensically important 0-1023. User ports 1024-49151 Private 49152-last Cheatsheets for known ports here.

item.title: Hiding data
item.description: There lots of ways to hide data. ADS Alternate data streams. Specific for Windows. See more here.

item.title: Mobile Forensic Analysis Introduction
item.description: Tool leveling pyramid The methods are placed in the order from the easiest to the hardest and most time-consuming:

item.title: Forensics vs Pentest
item.description: Forensics vs Penetration testing These two fields are closely related, for you have to acquire the same knowledge but you are just using it differently.

item.title: How I Got Started With AWS
item.description: In this blog post I am exploring AWS services (EC2). I am currently using a Free Tier license and I am trying to learn how to use.

item.title: Todo
item.description: https://digitalforensicsurvivalpodcast.com/category/mac-forensics/ Logs - https://digitalforensicsurvivalpodcast.com/2016/11/22/dfsp-040-mac-log-files/ https://digitalforensicsurvivalpodcast.com/2016/12/27/dfsp-045-run-dma/ https://digitalforensicsurvivalpodcast.com/2016/11/08/dfsp-038-finder-sidebar-forensics/ https://digitalforensicsurvivalpodcast.com/2016/10/18/dfsp-035-recent-file-listings-on-a-mac/ https://digitalforensicsurvivalpodcast.com/2016/10/25/dfsp-036-icloud-forensic-evidence/ https://digitalforensicsurvivalpodcast.com/2016/10/11/dfsp-034-forensic-tools-for-your-mac/ https://digitalforensicsurvivalpodcast.com/2016/10/04/dfsp-033-plists-for-mac-triage/ https://digitalforensicsurvivalpodcast.com/2016/09/20/dfsp-031-mac-user-home-folder/ https://digitalforensicsurvivalpodcast.com/2016/09/27/dfsp-032-mac-formats-libraries-keychains/ https://digitalforensicsurvivalpodcast.com/2016/09/13/dfsp-030-os-x-spotlight/ https://digitalforensicsurvivalpodcast.com/2016/09/06/dfsp-029-mac-cooties/ https://digitalforensicsurvivalpodcast.

item.title: How I Have Built My Lab

item.title: Browser Forensics
item.description: In the process of investigating Toy PD case, I’ve realised I lack the information about different browser files.

item.title: Constitutional Implications
item.description: First Amendment Congress shall make no law respecting an establishment of religion, or prohibiting the free exercise thereof; or abridging the freedom of speech, or of the press; or the right of the people peaceably to assemble, and to petition the Government for a redress of grievances.

item.title: DFIR Intro
item.description: This is my lecture notes of DFIR intro course. As I learn more that was provided in the course, I’ll add a reference and data.

item.description: wg data Webmail & gaming chat Identify program use Intro Installed volatile on mac:

item.title: Prefetch Forensics
item.description: This is my course notes from Prefetch forensics series. Tools WinPrefetchView To view pretech files (decompressed as well) in GUI.

item.title: Privacy and Admissability of Evidence
item.description: There is some information that is considered privileged. This can be excluded from the legal search even if there is a warrant.

item.title: Timelines
item.description: If something was downloaded and the accessed in several days - then the suspect knew about this file.

item.title: Ios Reverse
item.description: During forensic analysis it’s not too rare to run into some suspicious application. In this article I’m going to learn to reverse engineer iOS applications.

item.title: Account Information
item.description: Password hashes MacOS sudo plutil -p /var/db/dslocal/nodes/Default/users/<usename>.plist To crack: Windows SAM hive Linux etc/passwd and etc/shadow.

item.title: Frida Crash Course
item.description: Intro Frida is cool Templates Empty If a method needs an argument of type Context, getApplicationContext() needs to be used.

item.title: Mac Artifacts
item.description: Todo https://docviewer.yandex.ru/view/0/?page=2&*%3D&lang=en https://blog.elcomsoft.com/2016/07/mac-os-forensics-attacking-filevault-2/ https://macforensicslab.com/2011/01/01/general-forensics-tips-mac-platform/ https://forensicswiki.xyz/wiki/index.php?title=Mac_OS_X https://github.com/pstirparo/mac4n6 https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DgiljzWXkzc&t=21s https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=5VKTaFBlMcE https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=FOpiDSAD-Yk https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/.DS_Store OSDF Conference 2020 - Apollo

item.title: Notifications
item.description: Where are notifications stored in each of the below systems? MacOS Windows Win10 Win8 Win7

item.title: Report Writing
item.description: The final report should contain the following: case summary (who requested, who’s in charge, principles involved, when did the incident occur, when was this report filed, what allegedly happened, copies of all legal authorizations to proceed); Authorizations (original request in writing, warrants, subpoenas, written permissions); procedural docs (inventory of items examined and chain of custody for each, tools used, timeline of procedures, list of people performing these procedures, hashed before and after); case notes (all logs extracted from the target system and from the forensic tools, a diagram of network where investigation occurred (sometimes)); what physical evidence were collected (videos, pics, copies of docs, interview transcripts); conclusion (ties all together, supports or refuses the original claim, does NOT prove guilt/innocence).

item.title: IOS Screen Time
item.description: Overview Address book 📖 Call history ☎️ SMS 💬 MMS 💬 🌄 Email ✉️ Web browser history Photos 📷 Videos 📹 Music 🎵 Documents 📃 Calendar 📆 Network communication 🌐 Maps 🗺️ Social networking 🦜 Deleted data 🗑️ https://www.

item.description: This article is a temp one. I’m going to collect information about APTs that I come across.

item.title: Methodology
item.description: Steps Discover Obtain and image or physical evidence to work with. For Android, to see installed apps and recent activity:

item.description: # oh my zsh bash themes sh -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/master/tools/install.sh)" # sh -c "$(fetch -o - https://raw.

item.title: IOS Loggs
item.description: About iOS logs here. Syslog According to Apple docs, NSLog now doesn’t write to syslog, therefore I didn’t find a syslog.

item.title: Ios Loggs
item.description: About iOS logs here. Syslog According to Apple docs, NSLog now doesn’t write to syslog, therefore I didn’t find a syslog.

item.title: Network Analysis Lab Setup
item.description: Analysing Traffic Dshell Installing sudo python3 -m pip install [tar_file] # option 1 pip install dshell # option 2.

item.title: BTFM
item.description: dshell # MacOSX path to directory with pyenv (example): /Users/[user_name]/.pyenv/versions/3.8.5/envs/python3/lib/python3.8/site-packages/dshell/ decode -l # list decoders decode -d [decoder_name] # decoder info # who talked to whom decode -d ip [capture_file_name].

item.title: Forensic Toolkit
item.description: Here I will collect my arsenal of tools for forensic analysis. Autopsy There is a good course from Basis Technology about there own tool, of course, and I am sure there are lot’s of other tutorials out there available for free, so, this is not meant to be complete user guide.

item.title: A Study in Black
item.description: Someone has violated corporate policy by watching porn using the corp PC. Do you have the authority?

item.title: Live Acquisition Steps
item.description: Any activity on a live-system is changing the state of it. About 96%, though, remains unchanged.

item.title: Mac Imaging
item.description: I’ve recently listened to DFSP podcast in which Steve Wahlen talked about different caveats in Mac forensics.

item.title: Mobile Acquisition Methods
item.description: In this article I’m studying different acquisition methods, tool available for each, pros and cons of each tool as well.

item.title: Offline Acquisition Steps
item.description: Prerequisites Live USB/CD? Process Connect external HDD or SSD Lauch some forensic distribution (for example, Paladin).

item.title: Vehicle Forensics
item.description: CMD42 Lock ATA locks on HDDs. Usually 2.5'' HDD, verify there is a lock with MHDD, PC 3000.

item.title: BTFM and RTFM iOS
item.description: This is a combination of BTFM and RTFM (Blue Team Field Manual and Red Team Field Manual) for iOS devices and applications.

item.title: Attacking Android
item.description: Attacking device For this attack there are several types of attacker: • MiM, i.e. a sniffer.

item.title: BTFM and RTFM Android
item.description: This is a combination of BTFM and RTFM (Blue Team Field Manual and Red Team Field Manual) for Android devices and applications.

item.title: Debugging
item.description: In this article I’m assembling all possible to anti-debugging techniques and how they can be curcumvented.

item.title: iOS Jailbreaking
item.description: Types of jailbreaking Tethered jailbreaks don’t persist through reboots, so re-applying jailbreaks requires the device to be connected (tethered) to a computer during every reboot.
